chunks.text: One morning, I woke up with a slight ache in my head.
My mother touched my face and said, “ You have a fever.
My eyes and nose felt a bit sharp.
“ Take your medicine and rest quietly,” my mother said gently.
In the afternoon, we went to a hospital nearby.
At the desk, a kind nurse welcomed us with a smile.
In the doctor 's room, the doctor placed his tool on my chest.
“ Please don't push yourself for a while,” he said.
When I got home, I took my medicine and slept.
By evening, all the pain was gone.
My mother 's warm soup sank gently into my cold body.
At night, I was back to my healthy self.
Looking at the stars outside my window
I brushed my teeth and went to bed earlier than usual.
“ Nothing is more important than health,” my father said quietly.
His words stayed in my heart today.
